result.name: Improved STL Validator ASCII/Binary
result.description: Validates ASCII or binary STL meshes for closure, non-manifold edges, degeneracy, orientation consistency, normals, area, and signed volume.

result.name: Improved STL Validator ASCII/Binary CLI
result.description: Validates a single ASCII or binary STL file from the command line and prints JSON suitability results.

args.content:
import os
import json
import importlib.util

spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('prov', 'stl_validator_provided.py')
mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
spec.loader.exec_module(mod)

out = {
    'cwd_files': sorted(os.listdir('.')),
    'stl_files': sorted([f for f in os.listdir('.') if f.lower().endswith('.stl')]),
    'provided_tool_limitations': [
        'ASCII-only open(text) parser; cannot reliably read binary STL',
        'Normal check only flags similarity < -0.99, so many incorrect normals pass',
        'No global orientation consistency check across adjacent faces',
        'No non-manifold edge detection beyond counting open edges',
        'Degenerate triangles are skipped in normal check and not reported as invalid',
        'No signed volume / watertight manufacturability assessment'
    ]
}

for f in out['stl_files']:
    try:
        out.setdefault('results', {})[f] = mod.parse_stl(f)
    except Exception as e:
        out.setdefault('results', {})[f] = {'error': str(e)}

print(json.dumps(out, indent=2, sort_keys=True))

result.stdout:
{
  "cwd_files": [
    "__pycache__",

    "stl_validator_provided.py"

    "ASCII-only open(text) parser; cannot reliably read binary STL",
    "Normal check only flags similarity < -0.99, so many incorrect normals pass",
    "No global orientation consistency check across adjacent faces",
    "No non-manifold edge detection beyond counting open edges",
    "Degenerate triangles are skipped in normal check and not reported as invalid",
    "No signed volume / watertight manufacturability assessment"
  ],
  "stl_files": []
}
